articles.title: 'Optional, protected, not searchable': WhatsApp answers FAQs on 'Username' feature
articles.description: WhatsApp is clarifying its upcoming username feature amidst government concerns about potential fraud and impersonation. The company assures users usernames are optional and will not be publicly searchable.  Existing popular names are reserved for legitimate owners.  However, the Indian government has asked Meta to halt the rollout until consultations are complete, citing risks of scams and identity spoofing.

articles.title: Why Iran fears another funeral disaster as Khamenei awaits burial for 4 months
articles.description: Iran is meticulously planning Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral to prevent the deadly crowd surges that marred previous burials. Lessons learned from the chaotic funerals of Ayatollah Khomeini and General Qasem Soleimani are shaping extensive security measures, including potential helicopter deployment and cross-border coordination with Iraq. The aim is to ensure Khamenei's final rites are a display of state control, not a repeat of past tragedies.

articles.title: Watch: 'Kya kar raha hai, yaar?' — Abhishek frustrated after Kishan mix-up
articles.description: Ishan Kishan suffered a second successive run-out dismissal after a mix-up with Abhishek Sharma in India's first T20I against England. A frustrated Abhishek was heard saying, "Tu pahuncha ki nahin pahuncha, kya kar raha hai yaar Ishan?" However, Abhishek recovered brilliantly with a 24-ball 59 as India posted 189/7. Persistent rain later forced the match to be abandoned.

articles.title: 'China trying to take over': Trump calls giving away Panama Canal 'stupid mistake'
articles.description: Former President Donald Trump voiced strong disapproval of the US handover of the Panama Canal, citing increased fees and alleged Chinese influence. He vowed to prevent Beijing's takeover. Trump also reiterated his stance on birthright citizenship, believing the Supreme Court erred, while celebrating a ruling that bolstered presidential removal powers for executive officials.

articles.title: Watch: 9-storey Kyiv apartment building partially collapses after Russian attack; residents trapped
articles.description: Kyiv endured a devastating overnight missile and drone assault, with a residential building partially collapsing and multiple fires erupting. This follows Ukraine's own long-range strikes on Russian targets. The escalating conflict sees both sides intensifying attacks, as casualty figures in the war reportedly surpass two million. Russia, meanwhile, reiterates warnings against continued US military aid to Ukraine.

articles.title: "Misogynist, homophobic and racist": Clavicular's runway debut sparks fresh debate over fashion's obsession with controversy
articles.description: 424's decision to have influencer Clavicular open its SS27 Paris Fashion Week show sparked widespread criticism from fashion commentators and social media users. Many argued the controversial casting overshadowed the collection itself and reflected fashion's growing reliance on viral moments. The backlash has fueled a broader debate over whether brands benefit from courting controversy or risk damaging their creative credibility by prioritising attention over design.

articles.title: "The day Charlie was k****d.....": Candace Owens' latest Charlie Kirk theory sparks fierce response from critics
articles.description: Candace Owens' latest comments about Charlie Kirk's death sparked renewed backlash after critics challenged her claims on social media. One viral post accused Owens of promoting a disputed "body double" theory and argued that available security footage contradicts her narrative. The exchange fueled fresh online debate, with critics urging people to rely on verified evidence and official court proceedings instead of unverified speculation surrounding the high-profile case.

articles.title: Stock Market Live Updates Today: BSE Sensex opens over 250 points up, Nifty50 above 24,050; crude oil prices continue to drop
articles.full_text: "There are some positive trends that are providing  near-term support and strength to the market. One, crude continues to fall with Brent below $71 now. This will further strengthen India’s macros and help in achieving higher growth while keeping inflation in check. Two, the auto sales numbers in June was strong with 24.1% growth in passenger vehicles. This Indicates that the demand momentum in the economy continues to be strong. Third, the AI trade in South Korea is weakening as indicated by the 11.5% correction in Kospi last month. This is positive for non-AI markets like India. Fourth, the FII selling is tapering off giving an upper hand to the DIIs. As a result of these positive trends Nifty is outperforming other markets now. For June, Nifty is up 2.5% while Kospi and Nasdaq are down 11.5% and 3% respectively. Whether India’s outperformance will continue or not will be determined to a large extent by the progress of the monsoon, which continues to be unsatisfactory. The strength in the banking segment and digital platform companies is likely to continue in anticipation of good Q1 results,” says VK Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist, Geojit Investments Limited. “Indian equity markets are expected to open on a positive note, with Gift Nifty trading at 24,205, up by 120 points. However, broader Asian markets are witnessing weakness in early trade, with sharp declines seen across major indices including the Nikkei 225 and KOSPI following overnight weakness in US technology stocks. Despite the cautious global backdrop, domestic markets are likely to draw support from improving technical structures and easing volatility conditions. In the previous session, the Nifty 50 rebounded strongly after two days of correction and gained around 0.6 percent, reclaiming the psychological 24,000 mark. The benchmark index continued to hold above its short- and medium-term moving averages, indicating that the broader trend remains constructive. Falling crude oil prices and stable volatility conditions also supported market sentiment. The gradual rise in support levels over recent sessions suggests that buying interest is emerging on declines, keeping the near-term outlook positive. The immediate hurdle for Nifty is placed in the 24,200–24,300 zone, which has emerged as a crucial resistance area over the past few sessions. A sustained breakout above this zone could trigger fresh buying momentum and open the path toward 24,500. On the downside, the 23,900–23,850 zone is expected to provide immediate support, and maintaining above this range will be important for preserving the current bullish structure. Derivatives data indicates a constructive undertone. The Nifty Put-Call Ratio (PCR) rose to 1.13 from 1.02, suggesting increased put writing activity and strengthening bullish sentiment among market participants. The elevated PCR reflects growing confidence that key support levels are likely to hold in the near term. The volatility indicator, India VIX, declined by 2.63 percent to 13.24, remaining below all major moving averages and signalling reduced uncertainty in the market. Lower volatility continues to support bullish sentiment, although a further decline in the VIX would provide additional comfort to market participants and strengthen the possibility of a sustained upmove. Option chain positioning continues to indicate strong support around the 24,000 strike, where significant put writing activity is visible. On the upside, call writers remain active in the 24,200–24,300 zone, making it the immediate resistance band. A decisive breakout above this zone may trigger short covering and accelerate upward momentum. Bank Nifty outperformed the broader market and advanced 0.85 percent, closing above the 58,000 mark. The index formed a bullish candle after the previous session's weakness and continued to trade comfortably above all key moving averages. The RSI improved to 63.22, while the MACD remained firmly in positive territory despite continued moderation in histogram strength. The overall structure remains bullish, although momentum indicators suggest some consolidation before the next major directional move. Immediate support is placed around 57,500–57,600, while resistance is seen near 58,500–58,700. Overall, the technical setup remains positive with a bullish undertone. The broader trend continues to favour the upside as long as the Nifty sustains above the 24,000 mark. While momentum indicators suggest ongoing consolidation, a decisive move above the 24,200–24,300 resistance zone could act as the catalyst for the next leg of the rally. The immediate trading range for Nifty is expected between 23,850 and 24,300, with a breakout beyond this range likely to determine the market's next directional move,” says Aakash Shah, Technical Research Analyst at Choice Broking. Index snapped its two sessions decline and closed higher by more than 0.5% to close around the 58,000 levels. In the daily chart it has formed a bullish candlesticks pattern with a higher high and a higher low highlighting buying demand around the recent breakout area. Going ahead, a move above last week high of 58,700 will open further upside towards 59,200 and 60,000 level in the coming weeks, being the 138.2% external retracement of the previous decline from 57,456 to 52,783. Failure to move above last week's high is likely to keep the index consolidating within the 57,000–58,500 range. The lows of the last two weeks are almost identical around the 57,000 mark, making it a crucial short-term support level. The overall bias remains positive, hence the current breather should be used to accumulate quality banking stocks in staggered manner for the next leg of up move. China Resources New Energy shares nearly tripled on their stock market debut in Shenzhen on Thursday after the wind and solar energy company raised 24.5 billion yuan ($3.61 billion) in Asia's largest initial public offering (IPO) of 2026 so far. The stock opened at 21.60 yuan, more than double its IPO price of 10.11 yuan. During early trade, it soared as much as 198%, triggering a temporary trading halt. The strong listing performance came despite weakness in the broader Chinese market and is being viewed as a key test of efforts to attract large domestic listings and encourage households to channel more savings into equities following a slowdown in IPO activity. China's blue-chip CSI300 index was trading nearly 2% lower in early morning trade. According to LSEG data, companies listing on the Shanghai, Shenzhen and Beijing stock exchanges raised $7.7 billion through A-share IPOs during the first half of the year, representing a 64.4% increase from the corresponding period last year. Including offshore listings, total funds raised by Chinese companies through IPOs almost doubled to $16.2 billion. Oil prices moved lower in early trading on Thursday after Qatar said indirect negotiations between the United States and Iran had made "positive progress". The talks, which concluded on Wednesday, focused on the Strait of Hormuz, the vital shipping route that handled around one-fifth of global oil supplies before the conflict. Brent crude futures fell 73 cents, or 1.02%, to $70.84 a barrel by 0102 GMT. US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude declined 83 cents, or 1.21%, to $67.75 a barrel. Both benchmark contracts had already dropped more than 1% in the previous session, settling at their lowest levels in four months. According to Reuters sources, US and Iranian negotiators spent two days in Doha discussing the movement of vessels through the Strait of Hormuz as well as the release of Iranian funds. Although shipping through the strait has partially resumed, tensions remain elevated after the two countries exchanged military strikes over the weekend following an Iranian attack on a cargo vessel. Nifty snapped its two sessions decline and closed higher by more than 0.5% to close around the 24,000 levels. In the daily chart it has formed a bullish candlesticks pattern which mostly remained contained inside previous session price range highlighting pullback after three sessions corrective decline. Buying demand on Wednesday session emerged from near the support area of 23,800 being the confluence of the previous gap area, last two weeks lows and the 20 & 50 days EMA. Going ahead, in the coming session a follow through strength above Wednesday high 24,050 will open upside towards 24,120 and 24,260 levels being the highs of current week and last week respectively. The index has immediate support around the 23,900-23,800 levels holding above the same will keep the bias positive and index to gradually head towards 24,500-24,600 levels in the coming weeks.

articles.title: Unable to withdraw PF or file claims? EPFO services unavailable for 7 days
articles.description: Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) services remain unavailable beyond the scheduled July 1 deadline due to a planned database consolidation and software upgrade. This outage, affecting member and employer portals, prevents access to crucial functions like claim submission and e-passbook. Meanwhile, a new EPF Scheme, 2026, has been notified, introducing digital reforms and simplified withdrawal rules, alongside initiatives to address past compliance issues.

articles.title: UPI volumes up 1.2%, rise to 76 crore
articles.description: Digital payment volumes saw a slight dip in June, with UPI transactions falling 2.1% month-on-month. Despite this, daily UPI activity remained robust, and year-on-year growth continues to impress. Other payment systems like FASTag and IMPS also experienced sequential declines, though yearly figures show mixed trends, indicating a dynamic digital payments landscape.

articles.title: Angelina Jolie's best action movies: 'Lara Croft', 'Salt' and more
articles.full_text: Before she became one of Hollywood's most celebrated dramatic actresses, Angelina Jolie was quietly redefining what a female action star could look and feel like on screen. Physically committed, effortlessly commanding, and capable of making even the most outlandish premise feel entirely believable, she built an action filmography that nobody else in her generation has come close to matching. Here are 6 of her finest. A high-octane car heist thriller directed by Dominic Sena, 'Gone in 60 Seconds' follows a legendary car thief who comes out of retirement to steal fifty cars in one night to save his brother's life, with Jolie playing Sway, his equally skilled and completely fearless partner. She brings a cool, electric energy to a film that is essentially built on testosterone, and every scene she shares with Nicolas Cage crackles with a competitive chemistry that the film uses brilliantly. It is a film that moves as fast as its title suggests, and it is all waiting on Prime Video. The film that turned Jolie into a genuine action icon, 'Lara Croft: Tomb Raider' follows the fearless aristocratic archaeologist as she races a shadowy secret society to recover an ancient artefact capable of bending time itself. Directed by Simon West, Jolie plays Lara as someone entirely at home in danger, bringing a physicality, wit, and complete absence of self-doubt to the character that made it feel genuinely iconic rather than simply lifted from a video game. She spent months training in martial arts, weapons handling, and motorcycle riding for the role, and every single hour of it shows on screen. Stream it on Paramount+ and relive the moment Jolie became an action legend. A married couple simultaneously discover that the other is a professional assassin and that they have each been hired to kill the other, with Jolie playing Jane Smith as the sharper, colder, and more lethal half of the partnership. Directed by Doug Liman, the film is one of the most stylish and purely entertaining action comedies of its decade, and the friction between Jolie's controlled precision and Brad Pitt's looser energy creates a chemistry that makes every scene crackle with something equal parts dangerous and irresistible. It is the rare action comedy that delivers fully on both, and it is streaming on Disney+ Hotstar, perfect for a night when you want something slick and endlessly watchable. A disaffected office worker discovers his father was the world's greatest assassin and is recruited into a secret fraternity of killers, with Jolie playing Fox, the fraternity's most feared and enigmatic operative. Directed by Timur Bekmambetov, the film is gleefully over-the-top and visually relentless, and Jolie brings a stillness and quiet authority to the role that makes her the most compelling presence in every room she enters. She says less than almost anyone in the film and commands more attention than all of them combined; it is on Netflix, and it is exactly as wild as it sounds. A CIA analyst is accused of being a Russian sleeper agent and goes on the run, leaving her colleagues and the audience entirely unsure of whose side she is actually on, with Jolie playing Evelyn Salt with a physical ferocity and emotional inscrutability that keeps you guessing until the very last frame. Directed by Phillip Noyce, the film was originally written for Tom Cruise, and it is impossible to imagine anyone else in the role after watching Jolie inhabit it with such complete, assured authority. It moves at a pace that never gives you a moment to catch your breath; queue it up on Netflix for an action night that earns every twist. Directed by Chloé Zhao, 'Eternals' follows a group of immortal beings who have secretly lived on Earth for thousands of years, with Jolie playing Thena, an ancient warrior goddess whose fractured mind makes her the most tragic and compelling figure in the ensemble. The film gave Jolie her first Marvel role, and she brought a fragile, haunted intensity to the action sequences that set Thena apart from every other fighter in the film. It is a Marvel film unlike any other and a quietly fascinating addition to her action filmography, streaming on Hotstar for anyone ready to see Jolie in the universe that has everything.

articles.title: The Jonas Brothers return in ‘Camp Rock 3’ trailer
articles.description: The Jonas Brothers are back in the spotlight with the 'Camp Rock 3' trailer, igniting fan frenzy. The new installment introduces fresh faces competing for a coveted opening act spot on Connect 3's sold-out tour. While the trailer hints at romance and rivalry, fans eagerly anticipate a potential Demi Lovato cameo, reminiscing about the beloved franchise's past.
articles.full_text: The TOI Entertainment Desk is a dynamic and dedicated team of journalists, working tirelessly to bring the pulse of the entertainment world straight to the readers of The Times of India. No red carpet goes unrolled, no stage goes dark - our team spans the globe, bringing you the latest scoops and insider insights from Bollywood to Hollywood, and every entertainment hotspot in between. We don't just report; we tell tales of stardom and stories untold. articles.title: Netizens react to Kangana Ranaut calling out Ram Kapoor on ‘Lock Upp’
articles.description: Kangana Ranaut made a guest appearance on 'Lock Upp' Season 2, directly confronting contestant Ram Kapoor about his 'brazen attitude' and suggesting he take the game more seriously. Kapoor defended his actions, but Ranaut urged him to improve. Netizens largely applauded Ranaut's fearless judgment, with many praising her hosting skills and recalling her impactful presence in the first season. Farah Khan also previously criticized Kapoor's behavior.

articles.title: SRK unveils new cricket stadium in Los Angeles | WATCH
articles.description: Superstar Shah Rukh Khan celebrated a big moment for cricket in the United States as the LA Knight Riders Cricket Ground in Pomona, California, officially opened.
articles.full_text: What started as a dream... turns into reality today. Welcoming you all to the Knight Riders Cricket Ground, LA. A place built not just for sports but also entertainment and for families... and memories that last forever. Special thanks to Jaybhai who has been too kind through... pic.twitter.com/5WEBSEHyOh- Shah Rukh Khan (@iamsrk) July 1, 2026
